acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:57, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:61, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:205 do not spend more than two consecutive sentences on free-standing general explanation before a new action, obstacle, or consequence occurs on the page; fail if those passages still read as detached briefing blocks.
problem: Scene pressure repeatedly pauses for generalized system briefings just after the chapter has already established the machinery in action. The proxy-file reset and the hold-room reset both step out of live conflict to explain what proxy work or cheap stock 'always' means, which loosens the Friday-clock vice instead of tightening it through the present obstacle.
rewrite_direction: Rework the explanatory passages beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:57, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:61, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:205 so each piece of world knowledge is attached to an immediate action, interruption, or decision already happening on the page. Fold the proxy-work judgment into Mira's reading of specific file fields during the call setup, and fold the bargain-line history into what the scan is proving right now instead of pausing for a mini-essay.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:79 includes at least one plainspoken reference to the pool-memory and one plainspoken reference to the old scar or surgery, and the phrase 'juvenile aquatic incident' is gone from the passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:95; fail if Mira's side of the call still stays entirely in claims-code abstractions.
problem: Mira's pressure voice in the proxy call drifts from dry, exact worker-speech into bureaucratic code that sounds closer to company procedure than to her own idiolect. Phrases like 'juvenile aquatic incident' and the stacked claims abstractions keep her competent, but they also over-formalize a live argument and narrow the register difference between Mira and Celia.
rewrite_direction: Revise Mira's lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:79, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:89, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:95, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:101 so she still sounds professionally trained but shifts into plainer, more pressured speech once the assistant resists. Replace the most memo-like abstractions with concrete nouns the scene has already earned: scar, old surgery, child, pool, drowning, memory.

acceptance_test: Pass if, when reading the revised paragraphs anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:113, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:137, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:183, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:261, at least three now end on concrete action, sensation, or interiority rather than a generalized statement about the city, the company, or the building; fail if three or more of those paragraphs still resolve into institutional aphorism.
problem: The chapter leans on the same prose move more than twice: after a vivid concrete beat, the paragraph caps itself with an aphoristic institutional-summary sentence about the city, the company, or the building. The individual lines are competent, but the recurrence becomes a tic and starts flattening surprise into thesis.
rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:113, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:137, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:183, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:261, replace at least three of the generalized cappers with scene-specific aftermath: a physical action, sensory residue, or a character-specific interior beat that only this moment can produce. Keep at most one institutional thesis landing across these cited paragraphs.

acceptance_test: Reading the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:253 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:285, a reviewer can no longer conclude that Tavi's named wetbench is already present before chapter_03; the chapter still shows a viable illicit repair space, but chapter_03 can remain the first clear introduction of the wetbench as a recurring object.
problem: The chapter materially introduces Tavi's wetbench before the continuity sheet's canonical introduction in chapter_03. At line 253 the room already contains "a portable wetbench," and line 285 treats "the wetbench" as an established fixture, creating object-state drift in a core recurring prop.
rewrite_direction: Revise the room-description span at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:253 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:285 so Tavi's room reads as an improvised one-off repair setup or portable kit rather than the canonical wetbench introduced later. Keep the illegal repair functional, but remove language that makes chapter_03's wetbench introduction impossible or redundant.

acceptance_test: Before the scene reaches snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:365, at least two revised paragraphs in the stretch beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:329 and passing through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:363 contain unmistakable Mira interiority (thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment) that changes how the bargaining reads. If the scene still plays as pure external dialogue/action until line 365, this remains a fail.
problem: The bench-side bargaining runs for roughly eighteen consecutive paragraphs with no Mira interiority before line 365 finally lets us back into her head. In a chapter whose strength is close-third pressure, this stretch goes transcript-flat exactly where trust, money, and criminal exposure are being negotiated.
rewrite_direction: Thread two or three brief interior beats into the negotiation stretch anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:329, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:349, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:363: Mira's bodily awareness of the badge in the room, her calculation of what Tavi's price test means, or her reaction to Nidia shutting down. Keep the pace, but restore close-third presence before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:365.

acceptance_test: In the revised cited passages, no more than two beats use the exact withholding frame "Mira almost..." or "Mira did not...," and only one standalone "I know." remains as Mira's reply. The remaining moments of repression must each read differently on the page rather than repeating the same syntax.
problem: Mira's emotional suppression keeps resolving through the same withholding formula: "Mira almost..." at lines 115, 165, and 551; "Mira did not..." at lines 133, 437, and 555; and the fallback reply "I know." at lines 115/201 and 485. Used this often in one chapter, the stress pattern stops reading as live character behavior and starts reading like a preloaded tic.
rewrite_direction: Rework the withholding beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:115, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:133, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:165, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:201, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:437, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:485, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:551, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:555 so at least several of them land through differentiated physical action, sharper occupational language, or scene-specific deflection rather than another almost/not/I-know beat.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:437 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:625 contains one fully dramatized late-night intake or near-break moment where the room could fail, a visible rule or operating practice changes because of that event, and the dawn desk at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:625 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:715 feels like direct fallout from that scene. Fail if Blue Hour still becomes an institution mainly by summary.
problem: The chapter's key state change from one-night favor to functioning neighborhood desk is mostly narrated in retrospective summary once the overnight rush starts. The endpoint lands, but the decisive conversion of Blue Hour into an institution arrives as a report rather than as a fully dramatized hinge scene.
rewrite_direction: Rebuild the overnight escalation beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:437 and carrying through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:625. Convert at least one of the summary blocks anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:551 into a live case or operational crisis that forces Mira, Tavi, and Jo to formalize rules in scene, so the desk reveal at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:625 and the MEMBERS flip at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:715 read as earned consequences rather than narration catching up.

acceptance_test: Pass if, before the dawn count at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:655, one shortage introduced near snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:149 materially alters a case: a reader can point to who got deferred, what risky substitution was used, or what promise Mira and Tavi could not honestly make because supplies were low. Fail if the inventory still reads scarce but no later decision is constrained by it.
problem: The chapter tells us the bench is understocked and good for only a few serious cases, but the overnight line never visibly forces a treatment compromise, postponement, or ugly workaround. Scarcity is present as premise and mood, not as operational pressure shaping what care they can actually deliver.
rewrite_direction: Cash out the shortages introduced at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:149 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:151 during the later queue around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:537 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:559. Make one named shortage of foam, sealant, sleeves, scanner time, or another concrete supply change a treatment plan, force an improvisation, or require a client to accept partial care.

acceptance_test: Pass if, across the revised cited beats, no more than one speaker uses the exact uncontracted 'That is ...' correction frame, and a cold read makes Jo, Tavi, and Mira sound distinguishable by syntax as well as attitude. Fail if multiple speakers still share the same corrective scaffold or the same stiff full-form cadence.
problem: Jo, Tavi, and Mira repeatedly land corrections through the same uncontracted 'That is ...' frame. Because the style bible wants high default contraction use and sharper idiolect separation, this recurring syntax starts to sound like a house voice rather than three distinct speakers under pressure.
rewrite_direction: Retune the correction beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:185, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:201, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:407, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:423, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:611. Keep the comic snap, but let Jo sound bossy and practical, let Tavi's objections loosen into his run-on repair-patter rhythm, and let Mira's pushback stay dry and process-shaped instead of matching Jo and Tavi word for word in sentence design.

acceptance_test: Pass if at least three of the five cited passages no longer hinge on an immediate 'it wasn't ... it was ...' or equivalent negation-then-redefinition pair, while the revised lines still communicate the same shift in Mira's understanding on a single read. Fail if the chapter keeps announcing new meaning through the same contrastive sentence template in most of those spots.
problem: The final third leans too often on the same negation-then-redefinition turn: the prose keeps arriving at meaning through versions of 'it wasn't X; it was Y.' Used this many times in one chapter, the move stops feeling like controlled emphasis and starts flattening cadence into a recognizable tic.
rewrite_direction: Vary the paragraph turns anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:513, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:585, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:617, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:627, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:701. Preserve the conceptual pivots, but recast most of them through image, action, or tighter free-indirect thought instead of the same immediate negation/redefinition sentence shape.

acceptance_test: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:17 in the revision, Lin's scar is explicitly on the left side, and no later line in this chapter places or implies the scar on the right. A reader comparing the chapter to the continuity sheet finds one consistent side only.
problem: Lin's signature scar is placed under the right ribs here, but the continuity sheet canonically identifies Lin's pale rib scar on the left side. Because this scar is the chapter's key identifying anomaly, the side swap is a direct continuity contradiction.
rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:17, change the scar placement to the left side and then re-check the nearby exam language so Mira and Tavi's handling still tracks the corrected anatomy. Preserve the scar's age, surgical neatness, and missing documentation; only the side should change.

acceptance_test: Across the revised beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:91, :183, :343, :403, :427, and :453, no more than two use the `It wasn't/It was` antithesis. At least four of those six moments must land through materially different sentence shapes while preserving the same narrative meaning.
problem: The chapter reuses the same antithetical emphasis pattern across multiple major beats: `It wasn't X. It was Y.` or near-variants. By the fourth and fifth occurrence, distinct emotional turns start landing in the same syntactic mold, which flattens prose that is otherwise much more alive than this fallback rhythm.
rewrite_direction: Rework the turns at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:343, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:403, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:427, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:453 so only the strongest one or two keep the antithetical structure. Recast the others through action, image, or free-indirect thought that fits the specific pressure of each moment instead of reusing the same negation-then-definition beat.

acceptance_test: In the revised spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:149, :185, :233, :375, :391, and :447, at least four reaction turns register through touch, posture, breath, sound, or interior inference rather than `looked` or `eyes` as the lead verb. No more than two of those six beats may still rely on gaze as the primary reaction cue.
problem: Emotional pivots keep defaulting to `looked`-led staging. That repeated gaze choreography becomes a prose tic, especially in a chapter whose style bible prioritizes touch, sound, and spatial pressure; too many turns are registered through who looks at whom instead of through more specific bodily or environmental cues.
rewrite_direction: Revise the reaction beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:149, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:185, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:233, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:375, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:391, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:447 so at least four of them pivot through non-ocular cues: pressure in Mira's hands, Tavi's bench habits, Lin's anchoring motions, room heat/noise, or a sharper interior inference. Keep scene geography clear, but stop using eye direction as the default hinge.

acceptance_test: PASS if, across the six cited recall beats, no more than two begin with an indefinite-article noun phrase and at least two use materially different sentence shapes while still revealing classed, ordinary lives; FAIL if the recalls still read as repeated variants of the same setup.
evidence: Lin's overlap recalls arrive in near-identical syntax at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:97, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:129, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:133, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:245, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:449.
problem: Lin's voice is supposed to be variable by design, but the recall beats keep opening with the same article-led noun phrase and sensory-fragment cadence. By the fifth and sixth repetition, the memories feel templated rather than startlingly alive, which flattens both Lin's idiolect and the chapter's discovery energy.
rewrite_direction: Revise the recall entrances at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:97, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:129, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:133, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:245, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:449 so no more than two memories open with an indefinite-article noun phrase. Keep the ordinary class detail, but let at least two recalls arrive through different syntax: interrupted speech, bodily reaction before language, or a memory that starts mid-action rather than with another label.

acceptance_test: PASS if, in the cited spans, no more than two primary reaction beats rely on look/watch/stare verbs and each remaining visual beat marks a distinct shift in leverage or recognition; FAIL if the revised passage still repeatedly stages emotion by having characters look at one another.
evidence: Recognition and pressure beats lean on visual staging at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:171, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:291, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:511, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:521, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:551.
problem: The chapter overuses look/watch/stare choreography as its default beat container. After enough repetitions, moments that should feel tense or intimate get reduced to characters visually checking one another, which flattens paragraph texture and makes the scene mechanics too visible.
rewrite_direction: Rework the reaction beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:171, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:291, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:511, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:521, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:551 so at least half of those beats are carried by non-visual pressure detail instead: object handling, posture changes, pain management, interrupted speech, or room interaction. Keep only the gaze beats that mark a unique leverage shift.

acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span gives Mira's conversion one clear internal articulation before the key handoff and the explicit verbal statement of permanence arrives only once at the climax; FAIL if the passage still explains the same moral turn in multiple near-synonymous beats before and during the key scene.
evidence: Mira's shift is restated across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:425, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:437, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:491, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:511, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:529, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:535.
problem: The final third keeps paraphrasing the same emotional turn: Lin is no longer temporary evidence, the space is really being made for a person, and Mira is moving from procedure to commitment. The arc is correct, but the chapter explains it too many times before and during the key handoff, which softens the climax instead of letting the concrete gesture land with full force.
rewrite_direction: Tighten the commitment sequence across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:425, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:437, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:491, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:511, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:529, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:535. Keep one internal articulation of Mira's cost, then let the nook transfer, the copied key, and the final promise do the work; cut or repurpose any earlier beat that restates the same temporary-versus-person idea without adding new pressure.

acceptance_test: In the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:449-457, Mira attempts a visible work action and is blocked on-page by a person or system; at least one observer reacts in scene; the access downgrade is no longer conveyed only by narrated timestamps.
problem: Right after the Yaret interview, the chapter compresses Mira's workplace demotion into timestamped summary beats instead of staging one live humiliation on the floor. Because the chapter's engine is concealment under audit, this summary softens the bodily and social pressure at the exact point where her loss of function should feel most immediate.
rewrite_direction: Expand the downgrade beat in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:449 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:457 into an enacted floor sequence. Give Mira a specific task attempt after the interview, show the badge/access failure in real time, and add one concrete coworker or compliance interaction before Celia's summons so the institutional squeeze is dramatized rather than only reported.

acceptance_test: After revision of the cited spans, 'There it was' does not appear anywhere in chapter 06, and no more than one 'not because..., but because...' scaffold remains in the chapter; in each cited span, the leverage shift is legible from action/dialogue without a summarizing label such as 'the difference between them' or 'Men like him.'
problem: The narration repeatedly stops to label the meaning of the exchange in the same abstract hinge shapes: 'a gate instead of a reply,' multiple 'There it was' reveals, 'Men like him...' generalization, and repeated 'not because..., but because...' scaffolds. Used once, these land; repeated across a single chapter, they start to feel like a default explanatory move and flatten surprise.
rewrite_direction: Rework the explanatory hinges in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:83, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:107, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:285, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:327, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:433, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:467, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:505. Replace several of them with concrete gesture, timing, or material detail that lets the reader infer the power shift, and vary the sentence architecture where a gloss remains.

acceptance_test: Across the cited spans in chapter 06, no more than three wafer-sensation callbacks remain, and each surviving callback produces a different observable effect on Mira's behavior or decision-making rather than simply restating that the wafer is still in her shoe.
problem: Once Mira hides the wafer in her shoe, the prose returns to the same foot-pressure reminder at nearly every scene turn. The motif is valid, but the near-identical recurrence makes it feel mechanical instead of escalating; the chapter starts reasserting the same fact rather than developing new consequences from it.
rewrite_direction: Trim and vary the wafer-reminder beats in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:277, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:287, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:357, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:435, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:485, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:567, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:573. Keep only the reminders that change the scene outcome, and make each surviving callback do distinct work such as altering her gait, sharpening fear of detection, or driving the final decision to go to Blue Hour.

acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:249-253 preserves the client's residue in the room and moves into the kiss without any sentence that explicitly says the sex is unrelated to Luz or otherwise morally clears Mira and Tavi in advance. FAIL if an exculpatory aside still appears before the kiss.
problem: Right before Mira kisses Tavi, the narration explicitly assures the reader that the desire is unrelated to the assaulted client who just left. That sentence works as a moral safety valve: it pre-clears the ethics of the scene instead of letting the recent injury, bodily residue, and compromised room remain active pressure. Given the constitution's sanitization standard, that is a craft failure, not a harmless clarification.
rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:249 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:251 so the room's contamination stays present without narratorial acquittal. Keep Luz's residue, the red-bin/sweet-rot context, and Mira's desire in the same frame, but remove the sentence that explicitly clears the sex of ethical contamination; let Mira choose the kiss under pressure rather than having the prose certify her innocence first.

acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:347-377 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:477-499 use no more than one 'He looked...' sentence total and render at least two of Tavi's state changes through nonvisual cues such as posture, breath, hand movement, or voice. PASS only if one of the comparison-led sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:189 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:253 is also rewritten into a more direct formulation. FAIL if the same scaffolds still carry consecutive emotional beats.
problem: The back half develops a noticeable sentence-engine tic. Major emotional turns keep arriving through the same comparison and observation scaffolds, especially repeated 'as if' constructions and repeated 'He looked...' entries into Tavi's state. The prose stays strong at the image level, but these recycled hinges start to flatten the chapter's most important turns into variations on the same move.
rewrite_direction: Rework the repeated scaffold sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:189, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:253, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:347, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:375, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:477, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:497. Keep Mira's close POV, but vary how she reads Tavi and the room: use posture, breath, hand pressure, voice break, spatial hesitation, or object interaction instead of repeatedly leaning on 'as if' comparison hinges or 'He looked...' sentence starts.

acceptance_test: Pass if, by the time the revised scene reaches the exit beat anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:423, a reader can point to at least one explicit line explaining why Paredes warns and leaves now instead of shutting Blue Hour down immediately. Fail if her departure still reads as authorial convenience rather than a legible choice inside her authority and the room's pressure.
problem: Paredes arrives on a complaint about crowding, bodily waste, and wet work, sees the crowd and hears equipment, then exits on a warning without the scene fully grounding why she chooses discretion over immediate closure or referral. Because the chapter's consequence depends on Blue Hour staying open long enough to become public, that softens institutional pressure at the exact point the scene should harden it.
rewrite_direction: Clarify the logic of Paredes's exit in the beats around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:215, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:365, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:423. Give her an on-page reason to stop at a warning now, such as a stated limit to her health-and-sanitation remit, a crowd-safety calculation, or an explicit promise of documented follow-up that preserves future danger.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:223 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:267 contains at least one unmistakable Mira thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment before the evaluative return at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:269. Fail if the whole turn still plays as 10+ paragraphs of exterior-only staging.
problem: The grief-circle pivot runs more than ten consecutive paragraphs largely as external blocking and spoken instruction before the chapter re-enters unmistakable Mira self-assessment. In a style bible that calls for tight third-person close, that stretch reads momentarily like staged coverage instead of lived perception.
rewrite_direction: Re-anchor Mira inside the sequence between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:223 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:267. Add one or two concrete beats of her sensation, calculation, or involuntary judgment while Lin takes control and while Paredes hesitates, so the leverage shift stays filtered through Mira rather than only through room choreography.

acceptance_test: Pass if no more than two of the targeted reaction beats still use the 'That got ...' construction and each surviving beat is differentiated by a specific consequence on the page. Fail if all three reactions remain framed through the same template or if the replacements merely paraphrase it.
problem: The chapter reuses the same crowd-reaction sentence template, 'That got ...,' at least three times. In a chapter built on comic escalation and group response, that visible repetition stops feeling like rhythm and starts feeling like a prose tic.
rewrite_direction: Recast at least one of the reaction beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:20, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:407 so the room's responses arrive through distinct physical or social consequences rather than the same lead-in formula each time.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised closing pages communicate Blue Hour's new public status through observable room-level detail, dialogue, or Mira's bodily reaction, with no generalized thesis sentence about 'the city' or 'the institution' in the targeted beats. Fail if the chapter still pauses there to summarize its own argument in abstract terms.
problem: Several late-page sentences step out of Mira's concrete noticing into thesis-like explanation about 'the city,' 'the institution,' and what people carry home. Those abstractions explain the chapter's social meaning instead of trusting the room, the notes, and the posted hours to imply it, which deflates momentum near the close.
rewrite_direction: Revise the reflective beats around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:521, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:571, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:589 to keep the insight tied to immediate objects, bodies, and Mira's own response. acceptance_test: Pass if the passage between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:285 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:297 contains no bare noun-phrase sentences like "Debt pool." outside dialogue or explicitly presented document text; fail if the revised narration still relies on fragment-only summary lines to convey the lot contents.
problem: The terminal-lot beat drops into bare noun-phrase fragments ("Hospital rental spillover. Debt pool. Resort return. Wash waiver. Recirculate.") that read as clipped narration rather than rendered fiction. That violates the constitution's explicit ban on elliptical clipping in narration and briefly makes the chapter sound like process shorthand instead of Mira's finished close-third perception.
rewrite_direction: Rework the lot-review sequence around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:285, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:287, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:295 so the staccato recognition arrives either as clearly marked on-screen file text or as grammatically complete narration anchored to Mira's reading of the terminal. acceptance_test: Pass if no more than two of the cited spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:73, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:283, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:375, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:441 still use an explicit negation-correction construction; fail if three or more of those moments remain built on the same corrective formula.
problem: The chapter leans too often on the same negation-correction sentence pattern to define perception: "It was not a machine stillness. It was a braced one"; "The horror was not hidden..."; "The file did not read like a failure report. It read like manufacturing instructions"; "It did not look menacing. That was the trick"; "They were not enough for a tribunal... They were enough for her." Once this corrective syntax appears this many times, it stops feeling like a chosen rhythm and starts reading as autopilot.
rewrite_direction: Vary the perception turns at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:73, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:283, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:375, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:441 so at least two of those beats land through direct image, consequence, or metaphor on the first pass instead of the recurring "not X / actually Y" reversal. problem: The chapter repeatedly leans on explanatory emphasis formulas at major turns: summary tags such as "That was..." and "There it was," plus negation-then-correction beats like "It did not... It..." and "That was not... exactly." Because these arrive on top of already strong sensory and dramatic material, the prose starts telling the reader how to weight the moment instead of letting image, gesture, and scene pressure carry it. The accumulation is noticeable enough to register as a chapter-level tic.

acceptance_test: Across the revised sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:65, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:273, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:297, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:311, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:391, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:397, no more than one beat still relies on a leading 'not X' correction, and each targeted line can be understood without needing a second clause to reveal what the narration actually means.
